title: Context, Cancellation, Backpressure, Structured Concurrency, and Leaks · Systems Transfer
summary: A request launches several operations; one fails, one blocks, one outlives the caller, and overload grows without bound. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a cancelable bounded worker group with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pass context as the first operation parameter across call boundaries and avoid storing it as durable domain state.
Derive, propagate, call, and observe cancellation, deadlines, timeouts, and causes while releasing timer resources.
Use context values only for request-scoped cross-boundary metadata with collision-safe keys, not optional parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Context Propagation
steps.instruction:
lab case — Request 2 launches validation, lookup, and audit work under a 40ms deadline. One stage blocks after cancellation and the input queue grows. Bound concurrency, propagate cause, choose overload behavior, and prove no goroutine remains.
